top_sources.top_snippet: Think of them as “on switches” that mimic the action of ghrelin, the hunger hormone, but in a way that’s highly selective. Ipamorelin, for example, spikes GH without spiking cortisol or prolactin. On
top_sources.top_context: Think of them as “on switches” that mimic the action of ghrelin, the hunger hormone, but in a way that’s highly selective. Ipamorelin, for example, spikes GH without spiking cortisol or prolactin. On the other side of the equation are GHRH analogues like CJC-1295 (no DAC), Tesamorelin, and Sermorelin. These mimic your natural growth hormone–releasing hormone. Instead of pressing the “on” button, they turn up the volume on the entire GH system. When you stack a GHRH analogue with a secretagogue (like CJC-1295 + Ipamorelin), you get a bigger, cleaner GH pulse than either alone. That GH
